title: Peppery Gooey Mediterranean Feta Dumplings
description: Hearty, veggiefriendly dumplings bursting with peppery roasted red pepper, creamy feta, and a hint of sweet dark brown sugar, perfect for meal prep.

ingredients.name: active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: proofed in warm water with 1 tsp sugar

instructions: In a small bowl, combine warm water, yeast and 1 tsp sugar; let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
Whisk flour and salt in a large mixing bowl; create a well in the center and pour in the yeast mixture.
Knead by hand or using a stand mixer with dough hook for 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with a clean towel and let rise in a warm spot for 1 hour or until doubled in size.
While the dough rises, heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium heat; sauté minced garlic until fragrant (about 30 seconds).
Add diced roasted pepper, chopped spinach, and oregano; cook until spinach wilts, about 3 minutes.
Fold in crumbled feta and season with salt and pepper; cook for another minute, then remove from heat.
Turn the risen dough onto a lightly floured surface; divide into 8 equal portions and roll each into a ball.
Place the dough balls into a large pot of simmering salted water; cook for 20 minutes or until they float and are cooked through.

Drain the dumplings and transfer to a serving dish; drizzle with the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil, dark brown sugar, lemon juice, and a pinch of red pepper flakes for a sweet‑spicy finish. Serve warm or store in the fridge for meal prep.
